title: Pseudarrhenatherum
intro: Pseudarrhenatherum is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, commonly known as false oat-grass. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Pseudarrhenatherum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pseudarrhenatherum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pseudarrhenatherum is classified as a genus within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and is a type of perennial grass. This classification is subject to change as new research emerges.
